AREX All-Stop from Hongik University Station → ICN Terminal 1 (Air Premia) — no transfer
Passport + boarding pass

items.name: Gyeongbokgung
items.body: The largest of the Joseon palaces, with the changing-of-the-guard ceremony and the banquet hall over a lotus pond. Summer hours run to about 18:30 (last entry 17:30); closed Tuesdays, and free if you're wearing a hanbok.

img.file: Gyeonghoeru (Royal Banquet Hall) at Gyeongbokgung Palace, Seoul.jpg

items.name: Bukchon Hanok Village
items.body: Lanes of restored hanok houses on the hillside between the palaces, framed against the modern skyline. It's a living neighbourhood — keep noise down and don't peer into homes.

img.file: Bukchon-ro 11-gil street with hanok houses and blue sky in Bukchon Hanok Village Seoul.jpg

items.name: N Seoul Tower (Namsan)
items.body: On the wooded peak of Namsan, with the best wraparound view of the city. Ride the cable car or a bus up rather than hiking in the heat; the observation deck is worth timing for sunset.

items.name: Dongdaemun Design Plaza
items.body: Zaha Hadid's vast silver, curving 'spaceship', best after dark when it's lit and the LED-rose field is on. Design shops, exhibitions and the surrounding night markets make it an easy evening.

img.file: Dongdaemun Design Plaza at night, Seoul, Korea.jpg

items.name: Cheonggyecheon
items.body: A restored stream cutting through the centre with walkways below street level — a cool, easy stroll and a good flat break between sights.

items.name: Insadong
items.body: A walkable lane of galleries, traditional craft shops and tea houses, a short hop from the palaces — a natural pairing with a Gyeongbokgung morning.

sections.title: Visiting tips
sections.body: Time the palaces around their closed days — Gyeongbokgung is shut Tuesdays, Changdeokgung Mondays — and pre-book the Changdeokgung Secret Garden, which only enters on timed guided walks. Bukchon is residential, so it's best early and quiet. And don't fight the July heat: treat the air-conditioned stops (DDP, COEX, museums) as the spine of the hottest afternoons.

sections.title: Your MSI weekend
sections.body:
The Mid-Season Invitational runs at the Daejeon Convention Center II (Hall 2), the first international League event ever held in Daejeon. Your tickets cover the Lower Bracket Final on Sat Jul 11 and the Grand Final on Sun Jul 12. Your Daejeon I-Hotel is ~130 m from the hall — a 2-minute walk — both days you stroll over.

What to bring:

  • Photo ID (passport) — tickets are name-tied and scanned at entry via the NOL World app
  • Bag no larger than 45.7 cm × 57 cm × 19 cm (~18" × 22" × 7.5"), must fit under your seat. DCC II has no bag check — oversized bags are turned away at the door with nowhere to leave them. The I-Hotel is 2 min away, so drop anything large before you walk over.
  • Outside food is allowed if sealed or in an appropriate container — saves money vs. arena concessions
  • Portable battery pack — phone needs to last all day for the NOL World ticket scan, live coverage, and GO Fest raids before/after

Prohibited (per the official Riot event policy): glass containers, cans, laser pens, air horns, banners or flags on poles, drones, selfie-sticks, laptops and tablets. Non-compliant items may be confiscated without return.

The merch queue is longest before the first match on Day 1 — arrive 60–90 min early if you want MSI merchandise. Day 2 line is shorter.

sections.title: Getting to the arena
sections.body: From Seoul Station, the KTX reaches Daejeon in about 50 minutes (book on the Korail app or korail.com; use Seoul Station, not Suseo/SRT). From Daejeon Station, a taxi to the arena is ~20 min, ≈₩11,000 (split three ways it's pocket change); the cheaper metro-plus-taxi combo runs ~25 min via Government Complex Daejeon (정부청사역). The arena is Daejeon Convention Center II (DCC II), 87 Expo-ro, Yuseong-gu — and your Daejeon I-Hotel (27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil) is ~130 m from it, a 2-minute walk, so you stroll to both matches and skip the post-match scramble entirely. While you're there, Sungsimdang (DCC 1F, 107 Expo-ro) is Daejeon's legendary bakery — fried soboro and chive breads; queue moves fast. EXPO Science Park is adjacent to DCC II with open plazas, PokéStops, and gyms — useful for GO Fest raiding windows between matches. It's the former 1993 World's Fair site: a large open-air campus with wide walkways, gardens, and permanent science pavilions spread across roughly 1 km. Free to walk through; no entry fee for the public outdoor areas. Expect sun with little shade cover — hat and water matter here.

sections.title: Hongdae after dark
sections.body:
The university district around Hongik is Seoul's youth-and-nightlife core — street buskers, arcade halls, themed cafés, late-night eats, and anime goods. It's the natural evening base for nights you're not at the arena. Completely foreigner-accessible: no Korean contacts needed, no documented door discrimination (unlike parts of Gangnam), and most restaurants stay open until 04:00.

Two bars worth knowing — both walkable from Hongik Univ. Station:

  • Mike's Cabin — 94-13 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu (~5-min walk from Exit 8). Opens 18:00. The go-to foreigner bar in Hongdae: reggaeton, pop, hip-hop, mixed local and international crowd. Cover ≈₩10,000 on weekends (≈ — confirm at door). Arrives before 22:30 to beat the queue.
  • Dokkaebi Party House — 22 Wausan-ro 17-gil, Seogyo-dong, Mapo-gu (~7-min walk from Hongik Univ. Station). One of Hongdae's biggest foreigner-friendly clubs: EDM to hip-hop, large mixed crowd. Fast-track entry + 1 free drink available via Klook in advance (≈ — verify cover on Klook before the trip).

Clubs fill at midnight and peak 01:00–03:00. Tuesday Jul 14 is the best night of this trip to stay late — no early alarm on Wednesday, and the flight home isn't until 21:30.

sections.title: PC bangs, arcades & LoL Park
sections.body: Korea's PC bangs — high-spec gaming cafés charging by the hour, often with food service — are everywhere and cheap; they're a rite of passage. Arcades lean into rhythm and crane games. League fans should check the LCK schedule: LoL Park in Jongno is the league's home studio/arena, and the Summer split resumes after MSI, so a live LCK match in Seoul may be on while you're there.

sections.title: Norebang, Han River & the ones Americans miss
sections.body:
Norebang (노래방) — private karaoke
Probably the most fun 2–3 hours in Seoul for a group. You rent a private room by the hour — typically ≈₩15,000–25,000/hour for a group room (≈, verify at the venue) — stock it with drinks from the lobby vending machine, and sing on a proper AV system with a catalogue of thousands including English titles. Completely private: no audience, no judgment, no one else watching. Hongdae is dense with noraebangs; look for neon signs or search Naver Map for 노래방. Walk-in is usually fine; late Friday and Saturday nights fill up, so arrive before midnight. Best fit for this trip: Tuesday evening (Jul 14) after the Secret Garden, since Wednesday's flight is in the evening and there's no early alarm.

Han River parks
Summer evenings, locals grab fried chicken from a nearby spot and eat on the grass by the river. Yeouido Hangang Park (Line 5, Yeouido Station, Exit 2–3) and Banpo Hangang Park (taxi ≈₩8,000–12,000 from Hongdae, or Line 2 to Dangsan then Line 9 to National Assembly (~20 min)) are the main ones. Bike rental available at both. The Banpo Bridge Rainbow Fountain runs free light-and-water shows daily at set times in summer (typically several showtimes between 19:00 and 22:00 — verify the current schedule on Naver Map or at the bridge before going). No entry fee, no language needed. A natural Monday evening option (Jul 13) after you're back from Daejeon and have done Gwangjang and DDP.

Korean corn dog (gamja hot dog)
A potato-battered hot dog — often with mozzarella inside, sometimes rolled in fries, dipped in your choice of sauces. Every street food zone in Myeongdong and Hongdae has them. ≈₩2,500–4,500 per piece (≈, check the stall). Messy and worth it.

Makgeolli (막걸리)
Creamy, lightly fizzy unfiltered rice wine; lower alcohol than soju and considerably less harsh. At a sit-down spot a ceramic jug is typically ≈₩8,000–15,000 (≈, verify). Pairs specifically with pajeon (scallion pancake) — Koreans associate the combination with rainy monsoon evenings, and after one July shower in Seoul you'll feel why immediately. Insadong has traditional makgeolli spots built into its alleys; Yeonnam-dong (walkable from Hongik Univ. Station Exit 3, ~5 min) has a more modern bar take.

Daiso
There are branches across Seoul including near Hongdae (search 'Daiso 홍대' on Naver Map). The Korean equivalent of a dollar store but actually good: travel accessories, skincare gadgets, character goods, snacks, phone cases — most things ₩1,000–5,000. Worth 30 minutes for cheap souvenirs and any trip supplies you realise you need. Completely self-service; no language required.

sections.title: Anime & game shopping
sections.body:
Animate Seoul (5F, AK& Mall, 188 Yanghwa-ro, Mapo-gu — Hongdae Station Exit 9; open ~11:00–22:00) is the largest anime specialist in the Hongdae area: figures, manga, Blu-ray, merchandise, and rotating pop-ups for seasonal titles. Yongsan i'Park Mall electronics floors carry games, trading cards, and figures — verify the current tenant mix at the mall, as renovations have shifted some vendors. SMTOWN COEX Artium (Gangnam; open 10:30–22:00) is the K-pop one-stop: SM artists' albums, photocards, limited merch, and a café. Myeongdong K-pop street around Myeongdong Station has comprehensive stock across all agencies; go early or skip if the weekend crowds are too much.

HYBE Insight (42 Hangang-daero, Yongsan-gu; BTS/HYBE fan museum) requires timed-entry tickets booked weeks in advance via the official app or site — book the moment you confirm your trip dates. No walk-in entry; no late arrivals. LoL Park (3F Gran Seoul Building, 33 Jong-ro, Jongno-gu; open 11:00–20:00; Jonggak Station Exit 1) is the LCK's home venue with a free-to-browse exhibition floor, Riot merch store, and PC café. LCK Summer split resumes after MSI — check the schedule for a live match while you're there.

sections.title: What to eat
sections.body:
Hit the obvious greats: Korean BBQ, chimaek (fried chicken and beer), tteokbokki, and market street food at Gwangjang — bindaetteok (mung-bean pancake), mayak gimbap, and live octopus if you're brave.

You're based in Hongdae/Mapo-gu — a young, local-leaning neighbourhood with excellent Korean BBQ within walking distance, buskers, street food stalls, and cafe culture. It's the better base for eating well without tourist-strip prices. For market experiences: Gwangjang Market (~25–30 min, Line 2 → Line 1) and Namdaemun (Gyeongui-Jungang Line → Seoul Station, 10 min walk). Myeongdong (K-beauty, street stalls) is ~30 min by subway — worth a half-day, not your daily default. Yongsan for beef and electronics (~15 min via Seoul Station). In July heat, naengmyeon (cold buckwheat noodles) and bingsu (shaved-ice dessert) are exactly what you want. Café culture is everywhere when you need air-conditioning.

sections.title: Ordering in Korean restaurants — the mechanics
sections.body:
More accessible than it looks from the street. The mechanics that make it easy:

  • Kiosks — most chain restaurants and many AYCE BBQ spots have self-ordering touchscreens. Tap the flag icon for English, then browse photo menus and add items. No conversation. At AYCE BBQ kiosks also handle your meat refill orders during the meal.
  • Picture menus — most mid-range sit-down restaurants have laminated picture menus or a QR code that opens photos. Point at what you want, hold up fingers for quantity.
  • Papago camera — for text-only menus, open Papago, tap the camera icon, and point at the page. It overlays live Korean-to-English translation without you typing anything.
  • Walk in and sit — if a table is open, take it. A server will come. Most casual Korean restaurants don't seat you formally. Hold up fingers to show group size if asked.
  • Banchan is automatic and free — you'll receive a set of small side dishes (kimchi, egg rolls, salads) without ordering them. Always free, always refillable, always included. Don't order them or pay for them separately.
  • Call button — most Korean restaurants have a button on the table to summon a server. Press it to order more or ask for the bill. If there isn't one, yeogi-yo! (여기요) gets attention — completely normal, not rude.
  • Paying — walk up to the register when you're done. Bills are rarely brought to the table. Card is accepted almost everywhere; some market stalls are cash-only.
  • The phrase that handles most orders: point at the menu and say igeo juseyo (이거 주세요 — this one, please).

What to order beyond BBQ — the overlooked ones:

  • Jjigae (stew) — doenjang jjigae (fermented soybean), kimchi jjigae, or sundubu jjigae (silken tofu). Served boiling in a stone pot, ≈₩8,000–12,000 (≈), usually comes with rice and banchan. Available everywhere; good breakfast or recovery food after a long day.
  • Dolsot bibimbap — rice and vegetables in a hot stone bowl with a raw egg that cooks on contact; crispy rice forms at the bottom. Mix everything together before eating. Safe, filling, ≈₩12,000–15,000 (≈).
  • Somaek — the default way to drink in Korea: pour a shot of soju into a glass of beer and drink together. Every table at every chicken or pork spot does it. No special ordering needed — just ask for soju and beer.

sections.title: Where to eat — picks near your route
sections.body:
All anchored to Melody House (Hongdae / Hongik University Station, Line 2). All prices ≈ — verify before you go.

Hongdae street food — the pedestrian zone and Wausan-ro strip around Melody House. Any evening, any night of the trip. No booking, no transit. Tteokbokki, hotteok, skewers, grilled corn — ≈₩2,000–5,000 per item.

AYCE Korean BBQ — budget pork belly (arrival-night pick)
Ungteori Meats (엉터리생고기) — 118 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu. From Melody House: ~15 min walk or 5-min taxi — same Mapo-gu neighbourhood. Open 11:00–23:00 daily. ≈₩14,800 pp; confirm on Creatrip. Fresh (not frozen) samgyeopsal. Walk-in; may queue on weekend evenings. Best on arrival night (Thu Jul 9) — close, no transit, easy on a jet-lagged group.

AYCE Korean BBQ — mid-range / beef option
MUSSO Hongdae (무쏘 홍대점) — 10 Jandari-ro, Mapo-gu. From Melody House: ~15 min walk. Open 24 hr ⚠ verify before going. ≈₩20,800–23,800 pp (beef, shrimp, yukhoe at higher tier). Walk-in. Good for late nights after returning from MSI or after the Jul 14 Hongdae send-off.

Chimaek (fried chicken + beer)
Chicken Baengi (치킨뱅이 홍대점) — 127 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu. From Melody House: ~15 min walk. Mon–Thu 16:00–01:00; Fri–Sun 14:00–01:00. ≈₩10,000–20,000 pp; confirm at venue. Garlic chicken is the standout. Walk-in; busy Fri/Sat after 20:00 — arrive early. Best on Day 5 (Sun Jul 12) as the post-Grand Final celebration (2 back from Daejeon + 1 already in Hongdae).

Cold noodles (July heat)
Yookssam Naengmyeon (육쌈냉면 홍대점) — 76 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu. From Melody House: ~15 min walk, Exit 9 of Hongik Univ. Station. Hours ⚠ not confirmed for 2026 — check yookssam.com. ≈₩15,000–18,000 pp (naengmyeon set with charcoal-grilled bulgogi). Walk-in for lunch; dinner can queue.

Gwangjang Market (광장시장) — 88 Changgyeonggung-ro, Jongno-gu. From Hongik University Station: Line 2 east to Dongdaemun H&C Park (~20 min), transfer to Line 1 one stop west to Jongno-5-ga, Exit 7–8 (~25–30 min total). Food vendors open from 09:00 (most vendors open Sundays — ⚠ individual stall hours vary, check before going). Cash only at most stalls. Bindaetteok ≈₩4,000, mayak gimbap ≈₩3,000. Best on Day 7 (Mon Jul 13) — weekday is emptier. Walk-in only.

Bingsu
Sulbing (설빙) — search '설빙 홍대' on Kakao Map for the nearest branch to Melody House. Open ~12:00–24:00. Injeolmi bingsu ≈₩8,900; mango/melon ≈₩14,900–15,900; confirm on sulbing.com. No booking needed. Perfect mid-afternoon on any hot day.

Daejeon — near DCC II (Expo district, Yuseong-gu)
Sungsimdang DCC Branch — 107 Expo-ro, Yuseong-gu, on the 1F of the convention centre itself. Open ⚠ ~08:00–21:00 — verify on site. Legendary Daejeon bakery; fried soboro and chive breads. Walk-in; grab breakfast before the arena on Sat Jul 11.
Ossi Kalguksu Doryong — ~0.2 mi from DCC II (search Kakao Map). Highly-rated knife-cut noodle soup; local favourite. Open lunch/dinner ⚠ — check before going. ≈₩9,000–12,000 pp. Walk-in. Good pre-match dinner Sat Jul 11.
Cham-i-mat Gamjatang & Sundaeguk (Expo branch) — ~0.2 mi from DCC II. Pork-bone stew (gamjatang) and blood-sausage soup — hearty recovery food after a long event day. ≈₩9,000–13,000 pp. Open ⚠ — verify. Walk-in.
Big night out (Sun Jul 12 — second Daejeon night, no train to catch): Dunsan-dong (Seo-gu, ~15–20 min by taxi or Metro Line 1) is Daejeon's dining-and-nightlife district — galbi, chimaek and bars. Sinsuwon Galbi (2F, Gukje Bldg, 1007 Dunsan2-dong, Seo-gu ⚠ verify hours) is a well-known grill there; book ahead for a group on a weekend evening.

Convenience store — the underrated late-night move
GS25 and CU everywhere, open 24 h: triangle gimbap ~₩1,500, instant ramen ~₩1,500, egg salad sandwich ~₩2,500, tteokbokki cups. After a full GO Fest / MSI day, often the right call.

sections.title: Breakfast near Hongdae
sections.body:
Korea doesn't do big hotel-style breakfasts, but Hongdae has good options from 07:00–07:30:

  • Isaac Toast — multiple locations near Hongik University Station; look for the yellow signs on Eoulmadang-ro and the side streets off the main pedestrian zone. Opens ~07:00. Korean-style toasted egg sandwiches ~₩3,500–5,500 (≈); 5–10 min queue on weekdays, longer on weekends. Quick, cheap, no language needed — point at the menu pictures. The best fast breakfast before an early palace day.
  • Paris Baguette / Tous les Jours — both chains have branches within 5 min walk of Melody House. Opens ~08:00. Coffee, croissants, egg sandwiches, sweet pastries — reliable, air-conditioned, and fast. Good on days when you're heading somewhere early.
  • Cafes in Hongdae — the neighbourhood is dense with independent cafes that open from 08:00 (some earlier on weekends). Most serve egg-toast, bagels, or light breakfast sets alongside coffee. Look for small cafes a block off the main street — cheaper than the main drag and often very good.
  • Convenience store (GS25 / CU) — everywhere, open 24 h. Triangle gimbap ~₩1,500, egg-salad sandwich ~₩2,500, hot coffee from machine ~₩600. The pragmatic call on early departure days, post-late-night mornings, or any day you want to eat at Melody House. Both GS25 and CU have branches within a 1-min walk of Hongdae's main streets.

sections.title: Late-night food after midnight
sections.body:
Seoul's late-night food scene runs well past midnight — and in Hongdae, it's right outside the door.

  • Hongdae restaurant strip — most restaurants in Hongdae run until 04:00, some 24 hours. After clubs: ramen shops, pojangmacha tents (street-food stalls), and simya sikdang (late-night diners serving comfort Korean food from 22:00 to sunrise). Mapo-gu's designated pojangmacha zone near Hongdae concentrates the best street tents legally. No transit, no planning — just walk out and find it.
  • MUSSO Hongdae — open 24 hr ⚠ verify (listed in the picks section above); works as a late-night AYCE option after clubs close. ~15 min walk from Melody House.
  • Boseung Hoegwan (보승회관 명동점) — 13 Myeongdong 9-gil, Jung-gu, Seoul. Open 24 hours. The standby for post-midnight hangover soup (haejang-guk): pork rib soup, ramen, and white sliced pork. ≈₩10,000–20,000 per person (≈, verify on-site). 5-min walk from Euljiro 1-ga Station Exit 6. Worth the ~30-min trip from Hongdae if you specifically want haejang-guk; otherwise the Hongdae strip has you covered.
  • Convenience store (GS25 / CU) — always the reliable backup: instant ramen at a hot-water station, tteokbokki cups, kimbap, snacks. No closing time.

sections.title: Shopping in Seoul
sections.body:
You're based in Hongdae/Mapo-gu — youth fashion and street culture on your doorstep, with excellent streetwear within walking distance. By district from Hongik University Station: Myeongdong (~30 min, Line 2 east → Line 4) for K-beauty (Olive Young flagship, Innisfree, Etude House); Gangnam / COEX (~35 min, Gyeongui-Jungang → Seoul Station → Line 9) for malls and SMTOWN; Dongdaemun (~22 min, Line 2 east) for late-night shopping (open until 04:00); Insadong (~25 min, Gyeongui-Jungang → Seoul Station → Line 3 to Anguk) for traditional crafts and souvenirs. Tax-free shops offer a VAT refund on purchases over ₩15,000 — instant in-store or at the airport kiosk on departure.

Streetwear in Hongdae (walking distance from Melody House): Musinsa Standard flagship (144 Yanghwa-ro, Mapo-gu; open 11:00–21:00; near Exit 9) — Korean casualwear at a wide price range. The alley cluster around Wausan-ro (Mahagrid, SOUR, indie labels) rewards a slow browse — 10–15 min walk from Melody House. Gentle Monster Hongdae (54 Dokmak-ro 7-gil; open 12:00–22:00) is an art-installation eyewear store — free to walk through. Ader Error has moved to Seongsu (Seongdong-gu, Line 2 ~30 min); worth the trip if design-forward Korean fashion is your thing.

sections.title: Souvenirs — Korea
items: K-beauty from Olive Young (sheet masks, skincare)
Korean snacks (honey-butter chips, Pepero, Market O)
Kakao Friends / Line Friends character goods
A celadon or craft piece from Insadong

sections.title: Collectibles & chain merch
sections.body: For collectors: a Starbucks 'Been There' Seoul mug, and the Kakao Friends and Line Friends flagship stores (Hongdae and Gangnam) for character-brand exclusives you won't find at home.

sections.title: Quick reference — when you're unsure what to do
sections.body:
Open this when no one can agree on a plan.

  • Hungry, near Hongdae, any time: Yookssam Naengmyeon or Chicken Baengi (both walkable, no reservation). Cold noodles in July heat are excellent.
  • Want KBBQ tonight: Walk the side streets off Wausan-ro after 17:00 — a dozen AYCE spots, no reservations needed mid-week. Budget ≈₩15,000–25,000 pp.
  • Fastest possible meal: GS25 or CU — triangle gimbap (≈₩1,500), corn dog, instant ramen at the counter. Genuinely good and a Seoul experience.
  • Hot and need AC: any café, COEX Mall (Samseong Station, Line 2), or the underground Hongdae Shopping Street below Hongik University Station. Free to enter, browse for as long as you want.
  • Nothing to do in the afternoon: Han River — Line 5 to Yeouido Station, buy fried chicken from the strip at Exit 3–4, sit on the grass by the water. Free, always worth it.
  • Want to raid but don't want to sightsee: Hongdae and Sinchon are gym-dense — you can rack up 30+ raids without going underground. Drop a Lure at a café table and grind.
  • Evening with no plan: Hongdae pedestrian zone at 20:00 — buskers, street food, energy builds toward midnight. Walk 30 minutes and see what pulls you in.
  • After midnight: MUSSO (24-hr Korean BBQ, several Hongdae locations) or any GS25 with ramen. Mike's Cabin if someone wants a bar, no cover charge.
  • Daejeon: gap between events: Hanbat Arboretum (free, walkable from EXPO, great PokéStop density) or the EXPO Science Park grounds. Both have shade.

sections.title: Road of Legends 2026 — your Jul 9–10 window
sections.body:
Road of Legends runs Mon Jul 6 – Fri Jul 10 (ends 23:59 KST). You land Thursday Jul 9 and catch the final two days. Up to 9 free Raid Passes per day from gym discs. Pokémon caught from any raid Jul 6–12 may earn a Special Pokémon Background collectible.

Thursday Jul 9 (arrival day) — take it easy: 5-star bosses all day: Crowned Sword Zacian (Fairy/Steel) and Crowned Shield Zamazenta (Fighting/Steel). Both shiny-eligible (1/20), both 2–4 trainers. Raid Hour 18:00–19:00 KST. Mega Gengar Mega Raid Hour 19:00–20:00. You just flew 14 hours — use 2–3 passes if you're feeling fresh, save most for tomorrow. Both Crowned forms are weak only to Fire and Ground — same team handles both. See counter charts below.

Friday Jul 10 (palaces day) — the real evening: One of the best raiding evenings of the trip. Full plan in the day-by-day entry.

  • Raid Hour 18:00–19:00 KST — Origin Forme Dialga and Origin Forme Palkia. Both are the best Master League forms of their species (Roar of Time / Spacial Rend). Save 4–5 passes here.
  • Primal Raid Hour 19:00–20:00 KST — Primal Kyogre and Primal Groudon. Groudon (Ground/Fire) has a quadruple Water weakness; your Kyogre counters make it trivial. Coordinate 5+ trainers for Primal tier. Groudon is your best Xurkitree counter on Sunday — a priority XL-candy target. Save 3–4 passes here.

sections.title: Raid counter charts — Road of Legends Jul 9 & 10

bosses.name: Crowned Sword Zacian
bosses.note: Thursday Jul 9 all day + Raid Hour 18:00–19:00 KST. Steel typing neutralises all the usual Fairy counters (Ghost/Dark/Bug do nothing useful here). Bring the same Fire/Ground team for Zamazenta right after.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Fire and Ground only. Steel cancels out every type Fairy is normally weak to — no Ghost, Dark, or Bug. Fire hits harder overall (Reshiram, Mega Blaziken). Primal Groudon covers both weaknesses in one slot. This same team works identically for Crowned Shield Zamazenta.

bosses.name: Crowned Shield Zamazenta
bosses.note: Thursday Jul 9 all day + Raid Hour 18:00–19:00 KST. Fighting/Steel is an unusual type combination — Psychic and Fairy (usually great vs Fighting) are fully neutralised by the Steel typing. Identical weakness profile to Crowned Sword Zacian: Fire and Ground only.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Fire and Ground only. Steel neutralises the Fighting weakness to Psychic, Flying, and Fairy. Reuse the exact same team you built for Crowned Zacian — there's no need to swap anything.

bosses.name: Origin Forme Dialga
bosses.note: Friday Jul 10 all day + Raid Hour 18:00–19:00 KST. The best Dialga form for Master League — knows Roar of Time. High priority for XL Candy. Both Fighting and Ground work; Ground slightly favoured due to Primal Groudon coverage.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Fighting and Ground. Steel's Dragon resistance means Dragon-type attackers do nothing here. Primal Groudon (from earlier in the same Raid Hour) is the best single counter if you have it; Mega Garchomp is the accessible Mega lead. Terrakion and Keldeo lead the Fighting options.

bosses.name: Origin Forme Palkia
bosses.note: Friday Jul 10 all day + Raid Hour 18:00–19:00 KST. The best Palkia form for Master League — knows Spacial Rend. Dragon and Fairy both hit super-effectively. If you raided Rayquaza at GO Fest Day 1, bring it.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Dragon and Fairy. Water neutralises Dragon's usual Rock and Ice weaknesses. Dragon-type moves hit harder overall; Mega Salamence is the top Mega lead. Fairy picks (Xerneas, Mega Gardevoir) are great if Dragon is short on trainers — Fairy moves are unresisted here.

bosses.name: Primal Kyogre
bosses.shiny_note: Same shiny model as regular Kyogre (black/dark highlights)
bosses.trainers: 5–10 trainers (Primal tier has significantly more HP than 5-star)
bosses.note: Friday Jul 10 Primal Raid Hour 19:00–20:00 KST. Pure Water — Electric hits hardest. Kyogre is also the top counter for Primal Groudon in the same raid hour; stack Raid Passes between both Primals.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Grass and Electric. Electric moves deal slightly more damage overall. Zekrom (Fusion Bolt) is the single best counter; Shadow Raikou and Xurkitree are close behind. Primal tier — 5+ trainers recommended, more if under Level 35. Coordinate before the hour starts.

bosses.name: Primal Groudon
bosses.shiny_note: Same shiny model as regular Groudon (gold/yellow highlights)
bosses.note: Friday Jul 10 Primal Raid Hour 19:00–20:00 KST. Ground/Fire = quadruple Water weakness (the only type that hits both super-effectively). Your Kyogre literally counters this boss. Priority target: Primal Groudon is your best counter for Xurkitree on Sunday, and top-tier Ground attacker for Master League XL.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Water only — but it is a quadruple weakness (both Ground and Fire take 2.56× from Water in GO). Ice, Grass, and other types that usually hit Ground well are resisted by Fire. Bring nothing but Water. Kyogre (available in the same Raid Hour) is the single best attacker; Mega Swampert and Mega Gyarados are the top Mega options.

sections.title: GO Fest Global 2026 — what to know
sections.body:
Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Global runs Saturday Jul 11 and Sunday Jul 12, 10:00 a.m.–7:00 p.m. local time worldwide — not location-gated. This year's event is completely free for all trainers (no ticket purchase or payment needed). Everyone gets: Special Research leading to Zeraora (Mythical, Electric-type — first appearance in Pokémon GO; research does not expire), Branching Timed Research (choose Mega Mewtwo X or Y path), and all event bonuses below.

⚠ Re-verify in the final week. Niantic finalises exact habitat boss lists, Super Mega mechanics, and shiny rates only days before the event. Everything in this section reflects the official pre-announcement and LeekDuck's tracking as of June 2026 — re-check LeekDuck and the in-game news the week of Jul 6, and adjust your priority targets if the lineup shifts.

Bonuses active 10:00–19:00 KST both days: up to 9 free Raid Passes per day from Gym discs, boosted Shiny encounter rates (historically ~1/20 for all legendaries and Super Mega bosses), lure modules last 1 hour, Incense-exclusive spawns (Unown, Tropius, Bouffalant), Party Play active, type-themed Field Research refreshes hourly with each habitat.

Bonuses active all day both days (00:00–23:59): up to 6 Special Trades per day (normally 1), 50% reduced Stardust cost on all trades, open 50 Gifts per day.

Raid rotation — habitats cycle every 3 hours (this is the posted LeekDuck lineup; Mega + 5-star bosses rotate hourly within each window):
Sat: Stormfire Peaks 10–13 (Ice/Electric/Fire) → Astral Tides 13–16 (Psychic/Ghost/Water) → Dragonflight Summit 16–19 (Flying/Rock/Dragon)
Sun: Earthforged Domain 10–13 (Ground/Steel/Normal) → Verdant Anomaly 13–16 (Poison/Bug/Grass) → Twilight Battlefield 16–19 (Dark/Fairy/Fighting)
Super Mega all day: Mega Mewtwo X (Sat) / Mega Mewtwo Y (Sun) rotate through every habitat. Full boss lists are in the day-by-day entries and counter charts below.

Mega Mewtwo debut (Super Mega tier): Mega Mewtwo X on Saturday, Mega Mewtwo Y on Sunday — first appearances in Pokémon GO. Both have shiny available. Mewtwo caught from Mega X raids knows Counter (fast); Mewtwo from Mega Y raids knows Psystrike (charged). Build teams in advance — see counter charts.

Shadow Palkia is in Shadow Raids every weekend Jul 1–Aug 4 (including GO Fest weekend). Shiny available.

Road of Legends pre-event (Jul 6–10): You land Jul 9 and catch the last 2 days of this pre-event. Features Pokémon Background collectibles from raids and GO Pass rewards (Lucky Trinkets, Gold Bottle Caps, bonus XP/candy). Spin stops on Jul 9 arrival day and Jul 10 palaces day to collect.

Ultra Unlock (from Jul 13): Niantic confirmed post-GO Fest Ultra Unlock content starting Jul 13, details to be announced after the global challenge is complete during the event weekend. Check pokemongo.com after Jul 12.

  • Raid pass strategy: 9 free passes per day = 18 raids across the weekend. Saturday (2 of you): all 9 in Daejeon — Stormfire at EXPO Science Park (10–13), Astral Tides (13–16), Dragonflight until 16:30, then walk to the arena. No transit cutoff — you get the full Saturday window. Saturday (1 in Seoul): 9 passes independently across all three habitats in Seoul. Sunday (all 3 in Daejeon): 9 passes each — morning at EXPO/Hanbat Arboretum, Xurkitree in Verdant 13–16, then Twilight Battlefield around and after the GF (you stay the night, so no early exit).
  • Remote passes: Save for Jul 9–10 and Jul 13–14 Seoul days when you're not at the arena.

sections.title: Already did Copenhagen? Your Global divergence
sections.body:
You raided GO Fest 2026 Copenhagen in person (Fælledparken, Jun 12–14), so much of the Global headline content is a repeat for you. Here's what changes when you raid the Global weekend in Korea.

✅ Already done at Copenhagen — deprioritise at Global:

  • Zeraora — you earned it from the Copenhagen Special Research. Global runs the same Zeraora line; if you completed it there you won't receive a second one. Nothing to chase.
  • Mega Mewtwo X & Y — both appeared in Copenhagen's 1,000-trainer Super Mega finale, and your in-person Mewtwo learned both Counter and Psystrike (better than the single-move Mewtwos from Global X or Y raids). At Global they're only worth a pass if you're still missing the shiny or want more Mega Energy / XL candy — they drop from must-do to an optional shiny re-hunt.
  • Entei, Moltres (Stormfire, Sat) and Kyogre, Groudon (Dragonflight, Sat) — caught at Copenhagen, where you also got the Primal Kyogre/Groudon that outclass the Global 5-star forms. Re-raid only for a missing shiny or as raw counters (Groudon/Kyogre still counter Xurkitree).
  • Shiny Wash Rotom — an in-person Copenhagen exclusive, not in the Global event. Nothing to do here.

🔼 Fresh at Global — your real priorities now:

  • S — Xurkitree (Sun Verdant 13–16, Daejeon): the Asia-Pacific regional Ultra Beast, not in the Copenhagen pool and catchable only on this continent. The single most valuable target of your Global weekend.
  • The legendaries Copenhagen lacked (its 5-star pool was only Moltres/Mewtwo/Entei): Rayquaza, Reshiram/Zekrom/Kyurem (Sat Dragonflight); Giratina Origin, Dialga/Palkia, lake trio, Xerneas/Yveltal, Solgaleo/Lunala (Sat Astral); Therian Landorus/Thundurus, Heatran, Origin Dialga/Palkia, the Regi trio (Sun Earthforged); Latias/Latios, Cresselia, Darkrai, Cobalion/Terrakion/Virizion, Zacian, Zamazenta (Sun Twilight). Spend the bulk of your ~18 weekend passes here.
  • Incense regionals — Tropius & Bouffalant (plus all-form Unown): normally region-locked to other continents; Global incense pulls them in anywhere, so pop an Incense for easy dex fills.
  • Verdant collection (Sun 13–16): Deoxys (all 4 forms), Genesect (all 5 drives), Necrozma (fusion fodder), the Tapu quartet, Nihilego — none were at Copenhagen.

Net effect: skip Zeraora and the Mewtwos unless you want the shiny; make Xurkitree your Sunday must, and otherwise hoover up the legendaries and incense regionals Copenhagen never offered.

sections.title: Beyond raids — research, eggs & wild spawns (Global)
sections.body:
The Global weekend isn't only raids — here's the rest, and how much to care given your Copenhagen run.

  • Special Research → Zeraora: free for everyone who logs in, but it's the same line you completed in Copenhagen — likely nothing new for you (see the divergence note above).
  • Incense spawns (both days): all forms of Unown, Tropius, and Bouffalant. Tropius/Bouffalant are region-locked elsewhere, so burn an Incense each day for the Pokédex entries; lures last 1 hour during event hours — drop one near DCC II / EXPO Science Park on Sunday.
  • Wild spawns: each habitat's wild pool follows its type theme (Verdant = Poison/Bug/Grass, etc.). Niantic hadn't posted the exact Global wild list as of this writing — ⚠ check LeekDuck the week of Jul 6. Otherwise catch with an eye on the day's featured types for Stardust and XL.
  • Eggs: the Global egg pool wasn't detailed, and unlike Copenhagen (½ Hatch Distance + 1.5× Hatch Candy) the Global bonuses include no egg-distance discount — so don't burn incubators here; spend the time raiding.
  • Trades (all weekend): up to 6 Special Trades/day and 50% Stardust on every trade. With three of you, line up swaps for Xurkitree, the new legendaries, and any Copenhagen-vs-Korea dex gaps.

sections.title: Pre-GO Fest checklist
checklist: Pokémon storage to 400+ (transfer/release beforehand)
Item bag cleared: stock up on Max Revives, Ultra Balls, Golden Razz Berries
Lucky Eggs ready (use before evolving / before mass Stardust bonuses)
Star Pieces ready (use during raids and catches to maximise Stardust)
Silver Pinap Berries stocked (use on Legendaries you need Candy XL for)
Charged TMs ready — re-roll shadow Pokémon and new Mega moves on day 1
Purified Gems ready for Shadow Raids if any appear
9 free Raid Passes per day — remember to spin Gym discs, not just battle
Pokémon GO Plus / Gotcha charged and connected for auto-catches during downtime
Battery pack fully charged; portable charger in bag for the 9-hour event window
Branching Research choice made: Mega Mewtwo X (Psychic/Fighting) or Y (pure Psychic)
Teams pre-built for Mega Mewtwo X (Flying/Fairy counters) and Y (Ghost/Dark/Bug counters)

sections.title: Priority targets — which raids to spend passes on
sections.body:
⚠ Did GO Fest Copenhagen? See Your Global divergence above — it demotes Zeraora and the Mewtwos for you and promotes Xurkitree plus the legendaries Copenhagen lacked. The S/A/B tiers below are the general ranking.

9 free passes a day and a packed match schedule — spend them on debuts, the region-lock, and meta picks. Every GO Fest legendary 5★ and Super Mega is 1/20 shiny (the best odds in the game). Tiers below are tuned to your weekend: Saturday is Daejeon all day for 2 of you (all 3 habitats, LBF at 17:00), plus one person independently in Seoul; Sunday is Daejeon all day for all three of you (Grand Final 15:00).

  • S — do these no matter what:
    Mega Mewtwo X (Sat, every habitat incl. Daejeon) — global debut, best Psychic attacker; shiny. Weak to Flying/Fairy.
    Mega Mewtwo Y (Sun, every habitat) — global debut, even stronger Psychic; shiny. Weak to Ghost/Dark/Bug.
    Xurkitree (Sun Verdant 13–16, Daejeon) — Korea-only regional UB, top non-shadow Electric attacker; you can't get it at home. Raid it before the 15:00 GF. Weak to Ground.
  • A — high value if passes/time allow:
    Sat Dragonflight (Daejeon, around the LBF): Rayquaza (best F2P Dragon + Candy XL), Groudon (top Ground, and your Xurkitree counter), Kyogre (top Water), Reshiram/Zekrom (top Fire/Electric for ML), Mega Salamence.
    Sun Earthforged (morning): Therian Landorus (best Ground attacker), Therian Thundurus (top Electric), Heatran (top Fire/Steel), Origin Dialga/Palkia.
    Sun Twilight (around the GF): Latios (top Dragon), Darkrai (top Dark PvE), Zacian (top ML), Zamazenta (great GL/UL wall).
  • B — fill / collection: Giratina Origin (Sat Astral, top ML Ghost), Deoxys Attack (Sun Verdant, glass-cannon PvE), Necrozma (Sun Verdant, fusion fodder for Dusk Mane/Dawn Wings), Nihilego (Sun Verdant, GL/UL PvP), the Regi trio + Regigigas (Sun Earthforged), Tapu quartet (Sun Verdant, PvP Fairy).
  • Shadow Palkia runs in Shadow Raids all weekend (separate from the habitat rotation). Grab one if a gym is handy, but it persists through Aug 4 — not urgent.
  • Pass math: Sat — 3–5 on Mega Mewtwo X, the rest on Dragonflight in Daejeon. Sun — 3–4 on Mega Mewtwo Y, 2–3 on Xurkitree, the rest on Twilight around the GF.

Your 6-team for the debuts (lead a Mega to boost the lobby; exact moves are in the counter charts below):
Mega Mewtwo X — weak to Flying / Fairy: Mega Rayquaza (or Mega Pidgeot) · Shadow Moltres · Rayquaza · Mega Gardevoir · Xerneas · Zacian.
Mega Mewtwo Y — weak to Bug / Ghost / Dark: Mega Tyranitar (or Mega Gengar) · Dawn Wings Necrozma · Shadow Tyranitar · Shadow Hydreigon · Chandelure · Mega Absol.
No Mega of your own? A full lobby of the listed Shadow/non-Mega counters still clears either Super Mega with 6+ trainers — aim for a group, not a duo.

sections.title: Raid counter charts — GO Fest Global 2026

bosses.name: Mega Mewtwo X
bosses.shiny_note: Global debut — first appearance in Pokémon GO
bosses.trainers: 10–20 trainers (Super Mega tier — harder than standard Mega)
bosses.note: Saturday Jul 11 only — appears in every habitat, so you can raid it in Seoul in the morning and again near the arena in Daejeon. Branching Research lets you choose the X or Y encounter.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Flying and Fairy only. (In Pokémon GO, Psychic/Fighting takes neutral damage from Ghost, Dark and Bug — don't bring those.) Flying is the top damage type: Mega Rayquaza (Dragon Ascent) is best if you have it; otherwise lead Mega Pidgeot — which is in Saturday's rotation — or Mega Gardevoir. Shadow Moltres and Rayquaza head the non-Mega Flying picks; Xerneas and Zacian add strong Fairy.

bosses.name: Mega Mewtwo Y
bosses.shiny_note: Global debut — first appearance in Pokémon GO
bosses.note: Sunday Jul 12 only — appears in every habitat, so raid it morning and afternoon around the Grand Final. Pure Psychic — more counters available than Mega X.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Bug, Ghost, Dark. Ghost and Dark are the strongest damage types here. Dawn Wings Necrozma is the single best counter in the game; Mega Tyranitar and Mega Gengar round out the premium trio. Shadow Tyranitar or Shadow Hydreigon are excellent budget shadow leads.

bosses.name: Rayquaza
bosses.note: Saturday Dragonflight Summit (4–7 PM) — live in Daejeon around the LBF; raid near DCC II / EXPO Science Park. Top F2P Dragon attacker and Candy XL for Master League. (Mega Rayquaza is not a raid boss this event — use Mega Salamence as your Dragon Mega lead.)
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Ice (2×), Dragon, Fairy, Rock. Ice hits for double super-effective — always lead Ice. Standard 5-star difficulty; 2–3 high-level trainers handle it comfortably.

bosses.name: Kyogre
bosses.note: Saturday Dragonflight Summit (4–7 PM) — live in Daejeon around the LBF. Top Water attacker and Candy XL; doubles as a Groudon and Xurkitree counter.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Grass, Electric. Zekrom and Shadow Raikou are the top Electric picks; Roserade or Kartana for Grass. Zekrom's Fusion Bolt is the hardest-hitting charged move here.

bosses.name: Groudon
bosses.note: Saturday Dragonflight Summit (4–7 PM) — live in Daejeon around the LBF. Top Ground attacker, Candy XL for Master League, and your best Xurkitree counter on Sunday.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Water, Grass, Ice. Water hits hardest — Kyogre (in the same habitat) and Mega Swampert / Mega Gyarados lead. Kartana and Mega Sceptile bring Grass; Mamoswine and Galarian Darmanitan bring Ice.

bosses.name: Giratina (Origin Forme)
bosses.note: Saturday Astral Tides habitat (1–4 PM). Top PvP pick for Master League; worth stacking Candy XL.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Ghost, Ice, Dragon, Dark, Fairy. Ghost and Dragon hit hardest. Shadow Gengar with Lick + Shadow Ball is the accessible shadow lead. Dawn Wings Necrozma is premier.

bosses.name: Xurkitree
bosses.shiny_note: Korea is in the Asia-Pacific region where Xurkitree appears — region-locked Ultra Beast, you can't get it at home
bosses.note: Sunday Verdant Anomaly (1–4 PM), Daejeon — the Asia-Pacific regional Ultra Beast and a top non-shadow Electric attacker. Raid it before the 3 PM Grand Final; it's the single most location-exclusive thing on the weekend.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Ground only (pure Electric) — and Ground is immune to its Electric attacks, so a full Ground team takes almost no damage. Lead Mega Garchomp or Excadrill; Groudon (from Saturday) and Therian Landorus are premium.

bosses.name: Necrozma
bosses.note: Sunday Verdant Anomaly (1–4 PM). Base Necrozma — fuse with Solgaleo for Dusk Mane or Lunala for Dawn Wings (the game's best Ghost attacker). Worth a couple of raids as fusion fodder.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Bug, Ghost, Dark (pure Psychic — same profile as Mega Mewtwo Y, so reuse that team). Mega Gengar, Dawn Wings Necrozma and Mega Tyranitar are premier; Chandelure and Shadow Tyranitar are accessible.

bosses.name: Darkrai
bosses.note: Sunday Twilight Battlefield (4–7 PM). Strong PvE attacker; worth multiple shiny hunts.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Fighting, Bug, Fairy. Fighting-type attackers hit hardest. Mega Lopunny is the best Mega boost here; Lucario and Conkeldurr are accessible shadow leads.

bosses.name: Zacian (Hero of Many Battles)
bosses.note: Sunday Twilight Battlefield (4–7 PM). Top Master League meta pick; high priority.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Poison, Steel. Mega Beedrill and Nihilego are premier Poison picks; Metagross and Dialga cover Steel. Poison hits hardest — build your best Poison team.
